summary: Implemented the next DinoBrain OS goal step: task lifecycle reports now classify blockers into auto-close candidates versus manual repair classes, and a first-class deterministic RAG eval gate now records memory-on/off proxy lift, expected path recall, required context recall, provenance coverage, retrieval mode, hybrid ratio, and caveats. The current vault now shows status freshness healthy, lifecycle still needs_attention with 12 blockers, and RAG still needs_attention because hybrid_ratio is 0 with lexical fallback only.
decisions: Do not hide current lifecycle blockers; classify them as auto_close_candidate or manual repair classes so final readiness remains honest.
Do not claim full RAG/Ragas completion; add a deterministic RAG canary that fails current vault when dense hybrid retrieval is absent.
Make RAG eval freshness depend on golden data and retrieval indexes, including dense-vectors, wiki-index, and sqlite manifest.
Keep candidate/review data repo sync out of this app-code push because the data repo remains heavily dirty and needs scoped sync policy handling.
next_steps: Implement scoped task lifecycle settlement/repair for 6 auto-close candidates and 6 manual repair blockers.
Author explicit rag-golden.json and configure real dense vector generation/indexing so hybrid_ratio can reach the target without hand-made fixtures.
Add Observatory Korean bands for task lifecycle decisions and RAG eval status.
After scoped data policy checks, decide which data repo generated state artifacts should be committed and pushed.
search_queries: DinoBrain active goal finish-gate integrity real RAG eval current implementation
